findings.claim: Telnyx WebSocket server exists at telnyx.ts:115
findings.found: Line 115 is a state field `lastInboundMediaTimestampMs: number | null;`. The `export async function createTelnyxMediaStreamServer(` is at line 134.

Line 328 is `emitTelnyxMediaFrame(...)` (the no-chunk path). The reorder-buffer mechanism is `rememberTelnyxMediaChunk(...)` at line 330; the buffer field `inboundMediaReorderBuffer` is at line 114 and `flushTelnyxMediaReorderBuffer` at line 294/335.

summary: VE-04 bridge is substantially accurate. Verified SUPPORTED: twilio.ts:115 (server), :246 (clear on interrupt), :287/:290 (start validate), :303/:306/:308 (decode mu-law, resample, user.audio_received), :198/:201/:209 (resample/encode/send streamSid), :337 DTMF ignored; telnyx.ts:343 mark, :357 DTMF ignored; smartpbx.ts:107 server, :289 DTMF ignored. Absence claims all hold: no `dtmf.received` packet kind anywhere (only the three `if (event === "dtmf") return;` stubs), no mu-law passthrough, deepgram adapter has no mu-law/g711 encoding (edge transcode confirmed). All named implementation-target files exist (voice/src/packets.ts, packet-factories.ts, outbound-playout-pipeline.ts, voice-stt-deepgram/src/index.ts) and wireTelephonyOutboundPipeline + PacedPlayoutQueue back the 20ms pacing claim. Only two telnyx line-number imprecisions found; nothing that would cause the team to build the wrong thing or skip needed work.
